zh.Fermoy, Cork, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 科克費爾莫易
zh.Roche Castle, Roch, Pembroke, Pembrokesh: 威爾士布洛可爾布洛可
zh.lifeSummary: 當David De La Roche於大約1190年出生在威爾士布洛可爾布洛可時，他的父親Sir Adam Lord of Roch Castle De LaRoche31歲，母親Blandiane Marshal de Pembroke27歲。他於1219年與Joanna de Burgh在英國威爾斯布洛可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1283年在威爾士布洛可爾羅克享年95歲。

zh-hans.Fermoy, Cork, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 科克費爾莫易
zh-hans.Roche Castle, Roch, Pembroke, Pembrokesh: 威尔士布洛可爾布洛可
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当David De La Roche于大约1190年出生在威尔士布洛可爾布洛可时，他的父亲Sir Adam Lord of Roch Castle De LaRoche31岁，母亲Blandiane Marshal de Pembroke27岁。他于1219年与Joanna de Burgh在英国威尔斯布洛可爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1283年在威尔士布洛可爾羅克享年95岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Roche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aidan, Declan, Eamonn, Fergus, Fionnuala, Kevin Patrick, Liam.
Irish (Munster) and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Roach  . Compare   Roch  .

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a rocky outcrop or crag, from Old French roche ‘rock’. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the variant Laroche 2. Compare Deroche .

variants.name: Laroche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Normand, Pierre, Jacques, Laurent, Adrien, Cecile, Germain, Marcel.
French:
habitational name from any of numerous places in various parts of France named Laroche or La Roche, from Old French roche ‘rock’, with feminine definite article la.

variants.name: Larocque
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Henri, Marcel, Normand, Valmore, Yves, Achille, Amie, Donat, Emile.

French: habitational name from La Ro(c)que, the name of several places in various parts of France, from a regional variant of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche , compare Laroche ), with feminine definite article la. Compare Laroque .

Altered form of southern French Roquebrune or of its variant Rocquebrune: habitational name from Ro(c)quebrune, the name of several places in the southern part of France. The surname Roquebrune is rare in France, while the variant Rocquebrune is extinct there. Compare Larock , Lerocque , Rockburn , and Rocque 1.

variants.name: Rochet
meanings: French: diminutive of   Roche  , a topographic name meaning ‘rock’, or a habitational name from (Le) Rochet, the name of several places in various parts of France.
Catalan: of French origin (see above). This surname is also found in the Dominican Republic and Puerto Rico.

variants.name: Rochat
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique, Philippe, Pierre.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French roche ‘rock’ (see Roche 2), or a habitational name from Le Rochat, the name of several places in the eastern part of France.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: English:

of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Roquet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Camille.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche and Roque ), or a habitational name from (Le) Roquet, the name of several places in various parts of France.

variants.name: Rochez
meanings: Walloon and French: variant of   Roche   2 or   Rocher  .

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

en.lifeSummary: When David De La Roche was born about 1190, in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales, his father, Sir Adam Lord of Roch Castle De LaRoche, was 31 and his mother, Blandiane Marshal de Pembroke, was 27. He married Joanna de Burgh in 1219, in Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died about 1283, in Roch, Pembrokeshire, Wales, at the age of 95.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque David De La Roche est né environ 1190, à Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Galles, son père, Sir Adam Lord of Roch Castle De LaRoche, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Blandiane Marshal de Pembroke, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Joanna de Burgh en 1219, à Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé environ 1283, à Roch, Pembrokeshire, Galles, à l’âge de 95 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als David De La Roche um 1190 in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Adam Lord of Roch Castle De LaRoche 31 und seine Mutter Blandiane Marshal de Pembroke 27. Er hat 1219 in Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom Joanna de Burgh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um 1283 im Alter von 95 Jahren in Roch, Pembrokeshire, Wales.

Lui sposato Joanna de Burgh nel 1219, nel Pembrokeshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1283, nel Roch, Pembrokeshire, Galles, all'età di 95 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: David De La Rocheが約1190年にウェールズペンブロケシュアイアペンブロクで生まれた時，父Sir Adam Lord of Roch Castle De LaRocheは31歳，母Blandiane Marshal de Pembrokeは27歳であった。1219年にイギリスウェールズペンブロケシュアイアでJoanna de Burghと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は約1283年にウェールズペンブロケシュアイアロチで95歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: David De La Roche(이)가 대략 1190년 웨일스 p브로케실르 p브로크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Adam Lord of Roch Castle De LaRoche(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Blandiane Marshal de Pembroke(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1219년 영국 웨일스 p브로케실르에서 Joanna de Burgh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1283년 웨일스 p브로케실르 록h에서 95세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando David De La Roche nasceu aproximadamente 1190, em Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Gales, seu pai, Sir Adam Lord of Roch Castle De LaRoche, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Blandiane Marshal de Pembroke, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Joanna de Burgh em 1219, em Pembrokeshire, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1283, em Roch, Pembrokeshire, Gales, com 95 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда David De La Roche родился около 1190 г. в местности пемброк, пемброкешир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Sir Adam Lord of Roch Castle De LaRoche было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Blandiane Marshal de Pembroke, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Joanna de Burgh в 1219 г. в местности пемброкешир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер около 1283 г. в местности роч, пемброкешир, Уэльс в возрасте 95 лет.
